task: #62 Profile qwen35moe GGUF decode after fast prefill
summary: 512/128 qwen35moe GGUF decode remains below PARO-class target. One-step graph replay reaches ~49.864 tok/s (20.05 ms/token), about half of the ~100 tok/s floor and 57% below the PARO 116.05 tok/s row. Eager decode is only 6.47 tok/s, so graph replay is mandatory; however graph replay granularity from 1 to 16 steps does not materially change decode, indicating device kernel work rather than graph-launch overhead is the limiter. rocprof decode deltas show selected expert GEMVs, dense/shared Q8_0 GEMVs, lm_head Q6_K output projection, and full-attention/cache kernels dominate.

hardware.gpu: attached RX 7900 XTX / gfx1100 in this session; project default claims remain W7900/gfx1100 unless separately verified

software.hipcc_version:
HIP version: 7.13.60940-478a7a43c6
AMD clang version 23.0.0git (https://github.com/ROCm/llvm-project.git a3c1e65bfd98c7a76cafd24c34baf11f93e06be4+PATCHED:2506c552d8428e2cc1778bef048b20f818e06bb3)
Target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
Thread model: posix
InstalledDir: /home/lhl/miniforge3/envs/therock/lib/python3.12/site-packages/_rocm_sdk_devel/lib/llvm/bin

commands.wall_graph_granularity: For steps_per_replay in 2,4,8,16: same qwen35_gguf_bench.py command as graph1 with --graph-steps-per-replay ${s} and one measured run, JSON under /tmp/hipengine-task62/default-512-128-graph${s}-1run.json

rocprof.method: The 128-token graph-replay trace timed out under rocprofv3, so the retained profile uses a 512/128 eager decode trace for exact 128-token kernel dispatch counts plus a 512/16 graph-replay trace to verify the captured graph has the same per-token kernel DAG. Decode deltas subtract a 512-token prefill-only trace by kernel name.

failed_graph_trace_attempts.note: timed out without benchmark JSON; rocprof graph replay trace was reduced to 16 decode tokens for graph-internal kernel visibility, while 128-token eager trace supplies dispatch counts.
timed out without benchmark JSON; rocprof graph replay trace was reduced to 16 decode tokens for graph-internal kernel visibility, while 128-token eager trace supplies dispatch counts.

bottleneck_assessment.selected_expert_kernels: Selected expert Q4/Q5/Q6 row-GEMV kernels are the largest single decode family: 1279.237 ms over 128 eager-profiled tokens (37.58%, 9.99 ms/token, 120.0 dispatches/token). This remains a priority for grouped/tiled MoE.
bottleneck_assessment.shared_expert_q8_0: The Q8_0 GGUF kernel family (which includes shared-expert gate/up/down and other Q8_0 dense projections; rocprof names do not expose tensor names) is 1003.213 ms (29.47%, 7.84 ms/token, 250 dispatches/token). This is the second-largest family.
bottleneck_assessment.lm_head_output_projection: The tied Q6_K lm_head/output projection is one dispatch/token but 397.127 ms total (3.10 ms/token, 11.66%); argmax itself is only 1.731 ms total. Output projection bandwidth/quant kernel matters, argmax does not.
bottleneck_assessment.graph_replay_granularity: Graph replay is mandatory: eager wall decode is 6.470 tok/s (154.6 ms/token), while graph replay is 49.864 tok/s (20.1 ms/token), a 7.71x speedup. Increasing steps_per_replay from 1 to 16 keeps decode between 49.735 and 49.866 tok/s while capture time grows, so graph-launch granularity is not the current limiter.
bottleneck_assessment.host_overhead: Eager decode synchronizes and copies the sampled token each step and collapses to 6.47 tok/s. In graph replay, wall time is close to device kernel time; remaining host/graph overhead is not enough to close the 2.0x gap to 100 tok/s.
bottleneck_assessment.cache_attention: Full-attention/cache kernels are 331.536 ms (9.74%, 2.59 ms/token, 50 dispatches/token), material but smaller than MoE/dense/lm_head.
bottleneck_assessment.memory_pressure: 512/128 graph replay peaks at 20.886 GiB tracked / 21.346 GiB HIP sampled on a 23.984 GiB device, with no decode-time allocation growth beyond the tiny graph recording buffers. Memory pressure is not the immediate decode limiter, although headroom is too small for duplicate raw+packed expert residency.

blocker.summary: Decode is device-kernel dominated under graph replay and needs roughly 2.0x speedup to reach 100 tok/s / 2.33x to reach the retained PARO 116.05 tok/s. The largest actionable families are selected expert row-GEMV, Q8_0 dense/shared row-GEMV, and Q6_K lm_head/output projection; graph replay granularity and memory are not primary blockers at 512/128.
next_actions: Replace selected expert c=1 row-GEMV with grouped/tiled kernels or reuse the expert sidecar for decode so the 120 selected-expert dispatches/token shrink and reuse data.
Audit Q8_0 dense/shared call sites by tensor or add named profiling markers/wrapper counters; rocprof only exposes the kernel family, but it accounts for 250 dispatches/token.
Optimize or fuse the Q6_K lm_head/output projection and consider top-k/argmax integration; argmax alone is negligible.
Investigate full-attention/cache only after MoE/dense/lm_head, because it is under 10% of profiled decode kernel time at 512 context.
